subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/politics rebuilt from independent/multilateral sources (IMF Art IV/ECF/RSF, World Bank MPO, UN/OCHA/UNHCR, V-Dem, Freedom House 2026, RSF 2026, HRW, ACLED, Crisis Group) + international press (Reuters/AFP/Al Jazeera/France 24); junta/state media EXCLUDED on governance, rights, and security claims.

Data limitation: a data-poor conflict state; security figures and post-coup macro data are external estimates with reduced confidence; the CFA franc is euro-pegged via BCEAO with a French convertibility guarantee.

Standout drags: a July-2023 coup junta that detains ex-president Bazoum and dissolved all parties, a uranium nationalization (Somair/Orano) drawing adverse international rulings, and a Western security exit (US Agadez drone base and French troops gone) toward Russia; the oil-export ramp via the Benin pipeline is the rare upside. Net: negative.

economic.notes: An oil-driven growth ramp and CFA stability, offset by high debt distress, uranium-dispute risk, and post-coup financing strain.

fiscal_state.notes: At 'high' risk of debt distress with ~47-52% of GDP debt, arrears, and tight financing, though oil revenue and an IMF ECF arrangement provide some anchor. (IMF ECF/Art IV 2025; World Bank MPO)

monetary_independence.notes: The CFA-franc euro peg gives stability and a French convertibility guarantee but zero autonomy, under the regional BCEAO. (IMF/BCEAO)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The CFA franc is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Peg-anchored and receding toward the ceiling (~4% 2025, easing), elevated only by food/conflict shocks. (IMF; World Bank MPO)

banking_stability.notes: BCEAO-supervised and peg-backed, but strained by sovereign arrears and post-coup financing conditions. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Crude exports via the China-built Benin pipeline (>$2bn) plus uranium drive a growth ramp, but the Somair/Orano nationalization and an adverse ICSID ruling and recurring pipeline/Benin disputes cap upside. (S&P Global; Reuters; Manara/Discovery)

institutional.notes: A coup junta ruling by decree with detained ex-leadership, dissolved parties, and a repressed press.

rule_of_law.notes: Military rule since the July-2023 coup, all parties dissolved, ex-president Bazoum detained without trial (UN found the detention arbitrary). (Freedom House 2026; HRW; UN WGAD)

statistical_integrity.notes: Still IMF-engaged via ECF reviews, but post-coup disruption and oil-data novelty reduce reliability. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Weak and underfunded, strained by sanctions-era shocks and the security burden. (World Bank; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: Sharp post-coup decline, with journalists detained and foreign broadcasters curbed across the Sahel juntas. (RSF 2026; HRW)

political_social.notes: An entrenched junta and spreading insurgency, with large displacement and acute food insecurity.

political_stability.notes: Junta consolidated under Tchiani but illegitimate, with no transition timeline, dissolved parties, and a worsening western/Tillaberi insurgency. (Freedom House 2026; ICG; ACLED)

civil_liberties.notes: Parties dissolved, dissent suppressed, and the deposed president and associates detained. (Freedom House 2026; HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Hosts refugees from Mali/Burkina/Nigeria while producing displacement; protection strained by funding cuts. (UNHCR 2025; OCHA)

social_cohesion.notes: Ethnic and farmer-herder tensions amplified by ISGS/ISSP and JNIM violence in the border zones. (ACLED; ICG)

demographics.notes: Among the world's highest fertility, straining food, jobs, and services faster than the economy can absorb. (UN; World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: Pivoted from the West to Russia, exited ECOWAS into the AES, with a spreading insurgency and investment-dispute risk.

alliance_reliability.notes: Expelled US forces (Agadez base closed Aug-2024) and French troops, exited ECOWAS (Jan-2025) for the Russia-aligned AES - isolated from the West. (Stars and Stripes; France 24; Reuters)

conflict_involvement.notes: Rising ISGS/ISSP and JNIM violence in Tillaberi/the tri-border, less catastrophic than Mali/Burkina but worsening. (ACLED; ICG)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: ECOWAS sanctions lifted, but the Somair/Orano nationalization, an adverse ICSID ruling, and immobilized uranium raise expropriation/capital risk. (Reuters; Manara)

physical_practical.notes: Extreme climate vulnerability, very weak health, sparse infrastructure, and spreading insecurity.

climate.notes: Near ND-GAIN rock-bottom - extreme Sahel desertification/heat/drought, the fastest population growth, near-zero readiness. (ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Very weak with high child mortality and SAM, aid-dependent and stretched by population growth. (WHO/UNICEF; OCHA)

infrastructure.notes: Landlocked and sparse, with the new oil pipeline a bright spot but power access and roads very low. (World Bank; AfDB)

crime_safety.notes: Insecure in border regions with jihadist attacks, kidnapping, and banditry; Niamey relatively calmer. (ACLED; advisories)

disaster_insurance.notes: Negligible penetration against high climate and conflict exposure. (observed)

summary: Niger has been ruled by a junta under General Tchiani since the July 2023 coup; it detains deposed president Bazoum (the UN found the detention arbitrary), dissolved all political parties, and curbed the press. It expelled the US (closing the $110m Agadez drone base in August 2024) and French forces, exited ECOWAS for the Russia-aligned AES, and turned to Africa Corps. The economy has a genuine upside in the oil-export ramp through the China-built Benin pipeline plus CFA-anchored low inflation, but it sits at 'high' debt distress, and the nationalization of Orano's Somair uranium mine drew an adverse international ruling and left uranium stranded. With external data, a spreading Tillaberi insurgency, extreme climate vulnerability, and very weak health, the picture is negative, though somewhat less acute than Mali or Burkina.
